title: A Bride of a Summer’s Day

memes.label: Abruzzi
memes.description: A mountainous region in south-central Italy, mentioned in connection with the dialects of Calabria and as part of Sefton Culvers's travels through southern Italy in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (012-028)

memes.label: All Saints Church, Hastings
memes.description: The church in Hastings where Ida Culvers chose to be married and where her mother is buried in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. Its bells open the novel.

memes.label: Alpine Lakes
memes.description: The Alpine region — its lakes and mountains — mentioned as the intended honeymoon destination of Ida and Sefton Culvers in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (008-004)

memes.label: Alta Lauria
memes.description: A remote mountain hamlet in Calabria, southern Italy, where Sefton Culvers took refuge and where Ida later returned in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Arthur Glynde
memes.description: A poet and neighbour of the Culvers family in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. His romantic attachment to Juliet Culvers develops over the course of the novel.

memes.label: Aunt Sefton
memes.description: The aged, widowed mother of Sefton Culvers, living in Devon, whom Ida Culvers devotedly nursed for two years after her husband's death. (024-003)

memes.label: Avenue de l'Opéra
memes.description: The grand Parisian avenue from which Clive Redway is directed into the narrow streets leading to the Rue Vervien, marking the transition from public Paris to its hidden margins in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Beauty and Appearance
memes.description: Physical beauty — particularly Ida Culvers's inherited loveliness — functions as both social currency and narrative catalyst in Pirkis's novel, shaping marriage prospects, driving jealousy, and ultimately transcending the material world.

memes.label: Belgrave Square
memes.description: The London residence of Lord Culvers in Belgrave Square, the setting for key family scenes and the return from Paris in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Biarritz
memes.description: A fashionable seaside resort in southwestern France, mentioned in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day* as the destination of Ida Culvers's visit two years before the novel's events, during which she and Juliet agreed on a secret correspondence. (017-005)

memes.label: Calabria
memes.description: A mountainous region in southern Italy, setting of the novel's climax — home to Alta Lauria and the bandit Peter the Calabrian in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Caréno
memes.description: A tiny, earthquake-damaged village in the La Sila mountains of Calabria, serving as the last railway stop before the mule trek to Alta Lauria. (022-001)

memes.label: Carmelite Friars Church
memes.description: The church of the Carmelite Friars in the Rue Bellarmine, Paris, where the English priest Baldwin officiated — the site of a questionable second marriage ceremony central to the mystery of *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Champs Élysées
memes.description: The grand Parisian avenue where Clive Redway walks to clear his mind during the tense hours of the Paris investigation in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Chancery Lane
memes.description: A street in London, the address of Messrs. Hunt and Locke, the solicitors who placed newspaper advertisements seeking information about Ida Culvers's missing diamond brooch in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (005-004)

memes.label: Chantilly
memes.description: A French racecourse town near Paris where the young woman later known as Marie Schira was first noticed by Captain Sefton Culvers, her first name "Frenchified" upon her, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (015-059)

memes.label: Class and Social Mobility
memes.description: The novel's exploration of Victorian class structure and the possibilities and perils of social mobility, from Lady Culvers's rise to the Redway family's commercial ascent in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Class Tension in A Bride of a Summer's Day
memes.description: An analysis of how Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel uses the contrast between aristocratic and mercantile masculinity — embodied by Sefton Culvers and Clive Redway — to critique Victorian class ideology.

memes.label: Clive and Juliet's Engagement
memes.description: The engagement between Clive Redway and Juliet Culvers that frames the novel's romantic plot and is tested by the crisis of Ida's disappearance in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Clive Redway
memes.description: The affianced lover of Juliet Culvers in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. Earnest, devoted, and sometimes impatient, his engagement to Juliet provides a parallel romance that contrasts with Ida's troubled marriage.

memes.label: Clive Redway's Search for Ida
memes.description: Clive Redway's determined investigation in Paris to find the missing Ida Culvers, tracing clues from the Rue Vervien to the Rue Corot and ultimately uncovering Sefton Culvers's scheme in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Complete index of all themes, motifs, and social concepts explored in A Bride of a Summer's Day.

memes.label: Cosenza
memes.description: A city in Calabria, southern Italy, serving as the key transit point where travellers diverged from the railway into the mountain paths leading to Alta Lauria. (020-007)

memes.label: Culvers Family
memes.description: The aristocratic Culvers family of Dering Hall in Devon — their dynamics, hierarchy, and the tensions created by Lord Culvers's second marriage — form the social framework within which the novel's central marriage plot unfolds.

memes.label: Dering Hall
memes.description: The ancestral country estate of the Culvers family in *A Bride of a Summer's Day*, central to the family's identity and the location of key developments in the search for Ida.

memes.label: Dering
memes.description: The village near Dering Hall, the Culvers family's country seat, mentioned in connection with the family's English rural identity in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Devon
memes.description: English county where Captain Sefton Culvers's aged mother lived and where Ida spent her two-year widowhood in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Discovery of Sefton Culvers's Treachery
memes.description: The gradual revelation, through letters, testimony, and investigation, that Sefton Culvers planned to claim Ida's fortune through the marriage settlement's clause of reversion — the novel's central moral discovery in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Discovery of the Diamond Brooch
memes.description: The discovery of Ida Culvers's missing diamond brooch at a Carmelite Friars church in Paris, a key plot point that links the Paris investigation to the larger mystery of Ida's disappearance in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Disguise and Deception
memes.description: The recurring motif of disguised identity, assumed names, and deceptive appearances that structures the novel's moral geography in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Ditta Capelli
memes.description: A mule-driver in the Calabrian mountains in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. With his brother Andrea, he guides travellers through the mountain passes of La Sila.

memes.label: The English Aristocracy
memes.description: The novel's critical portrait of the late-Victorian English aristocracy — their wealth, anxieties, marriage strategies, and moral blind spots — in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: English vs Continental Morality in A Bride of a Summer's Day
memes.description: An analysis of how Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel uses the geographical contrast between Devon and Calabria to stage a moral argument about the relationship between place, law, and ethical accountability.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Complete index of all key plot events in A Bride of a Summer's Day, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Father Baldwin
memes.description: An English Catholic priest officiating at the Church of the Carmelite Friars in the Rue Bellarmine, Paris, who plays a crucial role in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day* by discovering Ida Culvers's diamond brooch in the offertory bag.

memes.label: Filial Piety and Maternal Legacy
memes.description: The motif of devotion to a dead mother, most powerfully expressed in Ida Culvers's choice of All Saints Church for her wedding and her visit to her mother's grave, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: First Lady Culvers
memes.description: The deceased first wife of George, fifth Baron Culvers, and mother of Ida and Juliet — a beautiful, vivacious woman whose "restless, excitable temperament" was inherited by her twin daughters in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Florence
memes.description: Italian city where Ida Culvers studied art and stayed before her deeper disappearance in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. A key location in the search for the missing bride.

memes.label: Francesca Xardez
memes.description: A wise-woman and healer of the Calabrian village near Alta Lauria in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. A person of local importance who aids Ida Culvers during her Italian exile.

memes.label: Gambling and Moral Ruin
memes.description: The theme of gambling as a path to moral and financial destruction, embodied in Sefton Culvers's descent from army officer to criminal schemer in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Gender and Agency in A Bride of a Summer's Day
memes.description: An analysis of how Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel explores female agency through the contrasting trajectories of Ida Culvers's passive suffering and Juliet Culvers's active intervention.

memes.label: George Johnson
memes.description: A minor character in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day* — John Skinner's associate ("chum") who could vouch for his whereabouts during the diamond brooch theft investigation. (016-036)

memes.label: Giorno
memes.description: The eldest son of Francesca Xardez, a vinedresser in Alta Lauria whose fellow workers paid for his passage to England to pursue Captain Sefton Culvers for his betrayal of Violante da Nava. (018-009)

memes.label: Glynde Lodge
memes.description: A house situated on the high-road between Ore and Hastings, lent to Lord Culvers for the wedding celebrations of his daughter Ida Culvers to Captain Sefton Culvers.

memes.label: Goody
memes.description: Former nurse and devoted family retainer of the Culvers household in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. Her cottage, known as "The Sanctuary," serves as a refuge for the Culvers sisters.

memes.label: Hastings
memes.description: Seaside town on the Sussex coast where Ida Culvers chose to be married and where All Saints Church stands in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Horse Guards
memes.description: The British Army administrative headquarters in London where Sefton Culvers formally resigned his commission before departing for Italy, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (001-061)

memes.label: Hôtel Bristol
memes.description: A Paris hotel where Clive Redway stays and where Lord Culvers and his party lodge during the Paris investigation in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Messrs. Hunt and Locke
memes.description: The firm of solicitors at Chancery Lane, London, who place the advertisement for Ida's missing diamond brooch and serve as intermediaries in the Paris investigation in *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.
aliases: Messrs. Hunt and Locke of Chancery Lane

memes.label: Ida and Clive's Reunion
memes.description: The reunion of Ida Culvers with Clive Redway after the resolution of her marriage to Sefton Culvers, marking the emotional conclusion of *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Ida and Sefton's Quarrels
memes.description: The series of desperate quarrels between Ida and Sefton Culvers during their brief, troubled marriage in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. These confrontations illuminate the toxic dynamics of a marriage built on financial calculation rather than love.

memes.label: Ida Culvers
memes.description: Elder daughter of George, fifth Baron Culvers, and twin sister of Juliet — a strong-willed beauty whose wedding to her cousin Captain Sefton Culvers opens the novel and whose subsequent disappearance drives the central mystery of Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Ida Culvers's Wedding Day
memes.description: The wedding of Ida Culvers to Captain Sefton Culvers at All Saints Church, Hastings — the inciting event of *A Bride of a Summer's Day* that sets in motion the novel's central mystery.

memes.label: Ida's Return and Restitution
memes.description: Ida Culvers's return from her two-year widowhood to make restitution to Violante, the child Sefton Culvers abandoned in Alta Lauria, the novel's culminating moral act in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Ida's Two-Year Widowhood
memes.description: The period of two years that Ida Culvers spent in rural seclusion in Devon following the death of Sefton Culvers in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. A narrative ellipsis that separates the catastrophe of her marriage from the novel's resolution.

memes.label: Identity and Naming
memes.description: The motif of names and naming as markers of identity, from Marie Schira's assumed name to Ida Culvers's transformation from bride to fugitive to widow, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Italy versus England
memes.description: The novel's sustained contrast between English order and Italian passion, embodied in the geographic polarity between Dering Hall and Alta Lauria, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Ivanoff
memes.description: A Russian proprietor of the boarding-house at 15 Rue Vervien, Paris, whose establishment serves as a resort for gamblers and adventurers and as Sefton Culvers's Paris residence in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: John Skinner
memes.description: The brother of Marie Schira and Miss Skinner, whose mercenary revelations about Sefton Culvers's scheme provide the final breakthrough in the Paris investigation in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Joshua Redway
memes.description: The father of Clive Redway and head of the Redway family's vast banking and mercantile empire, with offices in London, Liverpool, and New York.

memes.label: Juliet and Arthur Glynde's Engagement
memes.description: The resolution of Juliet Culvers's romantic storyline in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*, as she transfers her affections from Clive Redway to the patient poet Arthur Glynde.

memes.label: Juliet and Sefton's Confrontation
memes.description: The tense confrontation between Juliet Culvers and Sefton Culvers at Glynde Lodge after Ida's disappearance, in which Juliet's sharp intelligence challenges Sefton's evasions in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Juliet Culvers
memes.description: Twin sister of Ida Culvers and affianced lover of Clive Redway in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Justice and Retribution
memes.description: The novel's treatment of justice — both legal and moral, English and continental — and the question of whether wrongdoing meets its proper consequence in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Justice Without Law in A Bride of a Summer's Day
memes.description: An analysis of how the Calabrian subplot in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel functions as a form of extra-legal justice — a moral reckoning that English law cannot provide.

memes.label: La Sila
memes.description: Mountainous region of Calabria, southern Italy, whose remote pasture tablelands and crags form the setting of the novel's climactic journey in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

relations.label: The mountain journey through La Sila

memes.label: Lady Culvers
memes.description: The second wife of Lord George Culvers and stepmother to Ida and Juliet in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. Formerly a governess, her elevation to the peerage is a source of social tension.

memes.label: Landscape and Morality
memes.description: The novel's use of landscape and setting as expressions of moral character, from the seaside churchyard of Hastings to the wild Calabrian mountains, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Lombard Street
memes.description: The London financial district address of Messrs. Redway's offices, where Joshua Redway provides counsel to his son Clive about Ida's disappearance in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The metropolitan setting for much of the action in *A Bride of a Summer's Day*, including the Culvers family's town house in Belgrave Square and the law offices of Messrs. Hunt & Locke.

memes.label: Lord Culvers's Paris Journey
memes.description: Lord Culvers's journey to Paris to identify Ida's recovered diamond brooch and confront Sefton Culvers, a pivotal plot movement in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Lord George Culvers
memes.description: George, fifth Baron Culvers — father of Ida and Juliet Culvers, whose placid temperament and "odd fancies" shape the family dynamics in *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Love and Duty
memes.description: The novel's exploration of the tension between romantic love and social duty, and its ultimate resolution in favour of love tempered by moral obligation in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Madame Verdi
memes.description: The lady in Florence with whom Ida Culvers stayed during her flight from England in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. A figure of mystery whose knowledge of Ida's whereabouts is sought by the Culvers family.

memes.label: Marchese da Nava
memes.description: An elderly Italian nobleman of Alta Lauria, Calabria — widowed father of Violante and the chief landowner in the mountain hamlet where Sefton Culvers fell ill, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (018-005)

memes.label: margaret-pearson
memes.description: Redirect to [[goody|Goody]] — Margaret Pearson, devoted nurse of the Culvers household for over twenty years, Ida Culvers's childhood caretaker.

memes.label: Marie Schira
memes.description: A dying actress at the Théâtre Beaumarchais in Paris whose deathbed confession provides critical information about Sefton Culvers and Ida's fate in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Marriage, Property, and the Law
memes.description: The Victorian legal framework of marriage settlements and coverture that enables Sefton Culvers's scheme to claim Ida's fortune, a central theme in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: The Marriage Settlement
memes.description: The legal document governing Ida Culvers's marriage to Sefton Culvers — its clause providing for the reversion of her fortune on her childless death drives key plot developments in *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Martha Skinner
memes.description: Redirect to [[miss-skinner|Miss Skinner]] — Martha "Mattie" Skinner, Salvation Army sister of Marie Schira, who attended her dying sister in Paris.

memes.label: Miss Pigott
memes.description: The former governess and chaperon to Ida and Juliet Culvers who married Lord George Culvers, becoming the second Lady Culvers in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Miss Skinner
memes.description: The sister of Marie Schira — a member of a religious order summoned from England to attend her dying sister, who provides Clive Redway with crucial family history in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: The Mountain Journey Through La Sila
memes.description: The arduous journey through the Calabrian mountain range of La Sila toward Alta Lauria in the climactic chapters of Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. The physical ordeal mirrors the novel's moral pilgrimage.

memes.label: Mr. Redway
memes.description: Father of Clive Redway in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. A man of business-like reserve who becomes involved in the search for Ida Culvers after her disappearance.

memes.label: Mrs. Glynde
memes.description: The mother of Arthur Glynde, an energetic matchmaker and social figure whose lending of Glynde Lodge for Ida Culvers's wedding sets the novel's opening scene in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Naples
memes.description: Italian port city that serves as a transit hub and waypoint in the journey toward Calabria in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: The Newspaper Advertisement for the Brooch
memes.description: The newspaper advertisement placed by Messrs. Hunt and Locke for Ida Culvers's missing diamond brooch, the document that redirects the search from England to Paris in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: The Newspaper Advertisement and the Brooch's Discovery
memes.description: The placing of a newspaper advertisement for Ida's missing diamond brooch and its discovery in the offertory bag of a Paris church, the event that redirects the search from England to France in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Ore
memes.description: A village near Hastings, Sussex, on the high-road to which Glynde Lodge abuts, mentioned as a geographic waypoint in the opening chapters of Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: The Italian Organ-Boy
memes.description: An Italian street musician whose appearances at Glynde Lodge and 15 Rue Vervien provide a crucial link connecting Ida's disappearance to Sefton Culvers's Paris residence in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Organizations & Works Index
memes.description: Combined index of all organizations and literary works referenced in A Bride of a Summer's Day.

memes.label: Palais de Justice
memes.description: The Paris law courts where Lord Culvers and Clive Redway report Ida's disappearance to the Prefect of Police, marking the formal transition from private search to criminal investigation in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The French capital where key developments in the search for Ida Culvers unfold, including the discovery of the diamond brooch and the location of the Carmelite Friars church.

memes.label: Character Index
memes.description: Complete index of all characters in A Bride of a Summer's Day, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Peter the Calabrian
memes.description: A bandit operating in the mountainous region of La Sila, Calabria, whose legendary presence defined the lawless character of the territory where Sefton Culvers fell ill and encountered Violante da Nava.

memes.label: Phillips
memes.description: A lawyer involved in the legal and investigative response to Ida Culvers's disappearance in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Pippo
memes.description: The youngest son of Francesca Xardez, originally a goatherd in Alta Lauria who became the organ-boy in Hastings and served as a messenger in the plot to rescue Ida Culvers. (018-009)

relations.label: Saint Mary, Star of the Sea

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Complete index of all locations and settings in A Bride of a Summer's Day, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Porte Saint Martin
memes.description: The unfashionable Paris quarter where the Rue Corot is located and the Théâtre Beaumarchais operates, representing the city's theatrical and working-class margins in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Prefect of Police (Paris)
memes.description: The chief of the Paris police force, consulted by Clive Redway and Lord Culvers in their investigation of Ida Culvers's disappearance and the recovered diamond brooch.

memes.label: Red Cotton Nightcap Country
memes.description: A work by Robert Browning referenced in *A Bride of a Summer's Day* — Juliet Culvers pretends to read it while anxiously awaiting news about Ida.

memes.label: The Redway Family
memes.description: The Redway family of merchant-princes — Joshua Redway and his son Clive — representing commercial wealth, competence, and the rising bourgeoisie in contrast to the declining aristocracy in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Remorse and Expiation
memes.description: A central moral theme in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*, explored through characters who must confront the consequences of their actions and seek redemption through suffering and restitution.

memes.label: Royal Hussars
memes.description: The cavalry regiment of the British Army in which Sefton Culvers served before resigning his commission, mentioned in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (001-004)

memes.label: Rue Bellarmine
memes.description: The Paris street housing the Church of the Carmelite Friars and the residence of Father Baldwin, who discovers Ida Culvers's diamond brooch in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Rue Corot
memes.description: A narrow Paris street in the unfashionable quarter of the Porte Saint Martin, where the dying actress Marie Schira makes her deathbed confession in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Rue Vervien
memes.description: A Paris street whose No. 15, kept by the Russian Ivanoff, serves as Sefton Culvers's Paris residence and a centre of the novel's criminal demimonde in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Saint Leonard's Railway Station
memes.description: The railway station near Hastings where Ida Culvers's maid waited with luggage on the wedding day, mentioned as a key transit point in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Saint Mary, Star of the Sea
memes.description: A Catholic church in Hastings where Ida Culvers, after slipping away from All Saints churchyard, writes the hurried note to her father that diverts the wedding guests' search — a key location in her escape plan in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.
aliases: Church of Saint Mary Star of the Sea
St. Mary Star of the Sea

memes.label: Salvation Army
memes.description: The Protestant Christian charitable organization whose distinctive "poke-bonnet" identified Martha Skinner as a member when she attended her dying sister Marie Schira in Paris, in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. (015-028)

memes.label: Sefton Culvers
memes.description: Ida Culvers's cousin and new husband — a former officer in the Royal Hussars whose handsome appearance conceals a "pleasure-loving, vacillating nature" that will shape the novel's central conflict.

memes.label: Death of Sefton Culvers
memes.description: The death of Captain Sefton Culvers, which releases Ida Culvers from a marriage that had become a prison and enables the novel's resolution.

memes.label: Sefton Culvers's Flight to Italy
memes.description: Sefton Culvers's flight from England to the remote hamlet of Alta Lauria in Calabria following the collapse of his scheme, marking his retreat from English society to Italian isolation in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Social Scandal and Reputation
memes.description: The motif of social scandal and the terror of public exposure that governs the behaviour of the Culvers family after Ida's disappearance in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: St Leonard's
memes.description: Redirect to [[saint-leonards-station|Saint Leonard's Railway Station]].

memes.label: Stacy
memes.description: "Juliet Culvers's former maid of many years, whom Juliet regarded as the standard of loyalty: \"true as steel, pliable as whalebone.\" (007-070)"

memes.label: Sub Signo et Sub Rosa
memes.description: The secret Latin motto ("Under the sign and under the rose," meaning in confidence) engraved on the Culvers grandmother's amethyst seal, used by Juliet Culvers as a newspaper advertisement signal to communicate secretly with her twin sister Ida in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Summer Wedding
memes.description: "The central event that opens the novel: Ida Culvers marries Captain Sefton Culvers at All Saints Church, Hastings, on a summer's day — a wedding shaped by family history, social expectation, and personal will."

memes.label: Temper and Will
memes.description: The complex of strong will, quick temper, and eccentric determination that defines the Culvers women and drives the plot of *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: The Sanctuary
memes.description: Redirect to [[goody|Goody]] — Margaret Pearson's cottage on the Dering estate, used by the Culvers sisters as a refuge and secret communication point.

memes.label: Théâtre Beaumarchais
memes.description: The Paris theatre where Marie Schira performed as an actress of low comedy parts and where she sustained the fatal burns that lead to her deathbed confession in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Twin Bond
memes.description: The intense emotional connection between identical twin sisters Ida and Juliet Culvers — a bond that shapes the novel's central relationships, drives character choices, and ultimately provides the key to the mystery at the heart of the plot.

memes.label: Twin-Sister Dynamics
memes.description: The intense emotional bond between twin sisters Ida and Juliet Culvers, a relationship that provides the novel's emotional centre and drives its plot in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Vendetta
memes.description: The Calabrian tradition of blood-feud and retributive violence that threatens Ida Culvers during her exile in southern Italy, reflecting the clash between customary justice and legal order in the novel.

memes.label: Models of Victorian Masculinity
memes.description: The novel's range of masculine types — from the predatory Sefton Culvers to the patient Arthur Glynde to the shrewd Joshua Redway — offering a comparative study of Victorian manhood in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Victorian Mourning and Remembrance
memes.description: The Victorian culture of mourning that shapes the novel's treatment of death, memory, and the obligations of the living to the dead in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.

memes.label: Violante
memes.description: Nurse-child of Ida Culvers during her exile in Italy in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*. Devotedly attached to Ida, she receives an offer of marriage from a young Englishman.

memes.label: Death of Violante
memes.description: The death of Violante, a young Italian woman at Alta Lauria to whom Captain Sefton Culvers had made an offer of marriage, occurring shortly before Ida Culvers's arrival at the mountain hamlet.

memes.label: Violante Xardez
memes.description: Redirect to [[violante|Violante]] — the nurse-child of Ida Culvers during her exile in Italy, betrothed to Sefton Culvers under his Italian alias "Xardez."

memes.label: Women's Agency and Constraint
memes.description: The novel's exploration of the limited avenues for female agency in Victorian society and the various strategies women employ to navigate constraint in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 1891 novel *A Bride of a Summer's Day*.
